title: Boards arrived & Soldered
summary: The bare boards from JLC arrived flawlessly, despite a few small hiccups with the DigiKey order. One board is now fully assembled. Components were soldered on using a hot plate, an iron, and a hot air station.
body:
The boards arrived with no electrical issues. It looks like there were some issues with the footprints  and the solder mask but it only appeared on some boards and not on others so i think its just a weird edge case problem with jlc and my pad clearance being too close to each other, the pads are fine just the solder mask is a little weird on some of the ics that have super small pads. Aside from that the boards didnt have any noticible defects. \
\
I started soldering with the esp32 and a hot plate. I wanted to initially use an oven to reflow the pads under the esp32 but i didnt have access so a hotplate was the next best thing ic ould get my hands on. It was relatively simple, i just tinned pads on both sides and let it sit until i could see the surface tension pull the board onto the pads underneath.\
\
Moving onto the smaller ic's this was my first time soldering anything with a dfn package so it was a little different than expected, but with the hot air station it made is super easy. I stuck the solder paste on with the testencil and just hit everything with the hot air. I also wanted to use an oven instead in this step but as i had already put the esp on i didnt want to mess anything up with that so i took the safe route. \
\
As i was working on assembly i noticed that Digikey had forgotten to include one of my decoupling caps for the battery checker circuit up at the top. I shot them a quick email about it and they sent a replacement my wya. but unfortunately it arrived after i had already moved off of campus.\
as its a decoupling cap im not too worried and i think i can still test the board without it.\
\
Aside from that everything went pretty smoothly with the soldering and im excited to test the board, once i get my connectors from BetaFPV!
